sutta: remember ananda came to see the exalted 1 paid homage to exalted 1 and sat down to 1 side then he asked the buddha he said please sir may it be that amongst winning of concentration is of such a sort that in earth he is unperceptive of earth in water unperceptive of water in fire unperceptive of fire in air unperceptive of air in the realm of unbounded space unperceptive of it in the realm of unbounded consciousness unperceptive of it in the realm of nothingness and perceptive of it in the realm of neither perception or non-perception also aren't perceptive of it that in this world he is unaware of this unperceptive of this world in the world beyond his unperceptive of the world beyond that whatsoever is seen heard sensed cognized attained sought after taught over by mind of all that he is unperceptive and yet he perceives and the buddha said it may be so ananda amongst meaning of concentration may be of such a sort that in earth he is unperceptive of earth etc etc then remember ananda asked but sir in what way may amongst winning of concentration be of such a sort that of all that he is unperceptive and yet he is perceptive he perceives and then the buddha said here in ananda among his conscious dust this is the real this is the best namely the calming of all conditioners the rejection of every substrate the ending of craving the fading of interest stopping and cessation and ibana in such way ananda amongst winning of concentration may be of such a thought that in earth he is unperceptive of earth etc that in whatsoever is seen hurt sense cognized attained sought after thought out by mind of all that he is unperceptive and yet at the same time he perceives and that's the end of the sutta
commentary: it's quite an interesting suta here the member ananda is asking can a manga attain a state of concentration such that no [Music] he is in this world and yet no he is not perceptive of this world usually [Music] the buddha talks about feeling and perception if a person has feeling and perception it means he has consciousness he is conscious so if a person uh has no perception now it also means he has no consciousness but this in this case the buddha is talking about a person who has attained a state of concentration that he is not aware of this world at all he's not aware of he's not aware of whatever is seen sense heard cognized thought over etc and yet now at the same time he is conscious so the buddha said it is possible it is possible and he can attain this stage when he calms down all his conditioners and he has he's is disenchanted with everything in this world and he attains cessation and here he even says nibana [Music] so here basically what is being said now is that this state of nibana is of this sort if a person attains nibana a sixth consciousness will stop working this consciousness will stop working and yet there appears to be another type of consciousness another type of consciousness where that person is not even aware of himself so sometimes some monks say when a person attains nibana there is totally no consciousness and no any type not any type of consciousness at all but in certain sutas like in the vada sutta in the diga nikaya it is mentioned that in the state of nibana consciousness is boundless and luminous all brighter and yet in that state now there is no small no big no nama no rupa in other words when you have nama rupa you must have consciousness vinyana vinyana always comes with nama rupa because nama rupa is the object of consciousness mentality and materiality is always the object of consciousness so if you don't have nama rupa it means that you don't have consciousness but yet the buddha says there is a type of consciousness which is luminous and which is boundless so probably here is also referring to the same thing now we come to the last
